phase1: Map the component tree: identify container vs presentational components
Locate global state: Context, Redux, Zustand, Jotai — assess whether state scope is minimal
Identify re-render hot spots: large context consumers, missing memo/useMemo/useCallback
Document async data patterns: React Query, SWR, raw useEffect — note error/loading handling
Check for prop drilling deeper than 2-3 levels (consider lifting state or context)
Identify router (React Router, TanStack Router) usage and route-level code splitting
phase3: Split UI into small, single-responsibility components; keep render functions under 50 lines
Stabilize context values with useMemo to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all consumers
Use React Query or SWR for server state; avoid duplicating server state in local state
Apply React.memo to leaf components with stable props; use useCallback for event handlers passed as props
Lazy-load routes with React.lazy + Suspense; never bundle the entire app into one chunk
Colocate component styles, types, and tests in the same directory as the component
phase4: Test components with React Testing Library using user-centric queries (getByRole, getByText)
Mock network calls with MSW (Mock Service Worker) — test loading, success, and error states
Test custom hooks in isolation with renderHook
Measure re-render counts in performance-sensitive components with why-did-you-render
Run accessibility checks with jest-axe on key interactive components
